raw_consumer_statement:
I was overcharged {$72.00} by XXXX in the XXXX in XX/XX/XXXX. 

I contacted Citibank Visa to file a dispute. They never sent me the dispute forms. They finally wrote on XX/XX/XXXX saying they had closed my dispute without ever sending me the forms to file it. I phoned and they said yes it hadn't gone out, so they would reopen the complaint and send me the forms. Once I received the forms, I filled them out emailed them back with a detailed letter, receipts, photos, etc. on XXXX XXXX explaining the {$72.00} unauthorized overcharge. ( time stamped email and forms attached ) - On XX/XX/XXXX they sent a new form letter asking again for the information I sent previously, and to submit it by XX/XX/XXXX. 

- I duly filled out and emailed this form back to them on XX/XX/XXXX, as well as including the previously letter with all the evidence. ( time stamped email and forms attached ) - On XX/XX/XXXX they sent an email with a web link to a scan of a letter dated XX/XX/XXXX ( attached ) saying they had closed my complaint ( without addressing it ) since it was submitted too late?! But I submitted it everything as requested. Why close it when they had received my letters and their own forms by their self imposed deadlines? 

The consumer should not be punished for Citibank 's own incompetence and slowness in addressing the dispute. Simply refusing to address it is not a solution.
